cited_evidence.quote: We delivered our fifth straight quarter of greater than 50% year-over-year revenue growth, with each of our businesses growing significantly year-over-year and data center sales more than doubling.
cited_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly signals accelerating demand across all AMD segments, particularly the data center, which is the most strategically valued business — strongly positive for the demand dimension.

cited_evidence.quote: We expect revenue to be approximately $4.5 billion, plus or minus $100 million, an increase of approximately 39% year-over-year and approximately 4% sequentially.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Q4 guidance raised above prior implied levels, reflecting continued strong demand signals from customers across server and semi-custom, reinforcing the positive demand dimension weight.

cited_evidence.quote: On the revenue side, when we look at the sequential growth, this is about really supply chain optimization. We have been able to secure some additional supply given some of the work that we've been doing. We see strong demand across the board.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management explicitly cites strong broad-based demand as a driver of the raised Q4 guide, further supporting a high positive weight for the demand dimension.

demand.rationale: Record Q3 revenue driven by strong volume growth across data center, graphics, and semi-custom segments, with server revenue more than doubling YoY and management guiding Q4 revenues higher than prior implied guidance.

cited_evidence.quote: Gross margin was 48%, up 440 basis points from a year ago, driven by strong revenue mix and competitive products.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The 440bp YoY gross margin expansion reflects favorable pricing mix toward higher-ASP server and data center products, a clear positive signal for the pricing dimension.

cited_evidence.quote: Q4, it is up slightly based from our guidance, one point from Q3, and that's really product mix, higher margin from server, offset by semi-custom revenue also being higher going from Q3 to Q4.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management's Q4 gross margin guide of 49.5% (+1pp) confirms continued favorable mix shift toward servers, directly supporting a positive pricing/mix contribution.

cited_evidence.quote: For the full year 2021, we now expect revenue to increase approximately 65% over 2020, driven by growth across all businesses, up from the prior guidance of 60%.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The full-year revenue guidance raise also implicitly reflects stronger pricing power and mix, reinforcing the positive pricing dimension.

pricing.rationale: Gross margin expansion driven by richer product mix — particularly higher-margin server CPUs — with management guiding further margin improvement in Q4 to 49.5% and signaling ongoing progress into 2022.

cited_evidence.quote: We delivered our sixth straight quarter of record server processor revenue, as sales more than doubled year-over-year and grew by a significant double-digit percentage sequentially.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Six consecutive record server revenue quarters signals sustained competitive share gains against Intel, a core driver of AMD's positive competitive positioning and a key reason for the strong stock move.

cited_evidence.quote: I think Milan is extremely well-positioned, so we were very pleased with the adoption rate of Milan. We said that we expected it to grow faster than Rome, and it has.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Milan outpacing Rome's ramp confirms AMD's competitive execution is ahead of internal benchmarks, bolstering investor confidence in the competitive dimension.

cited_evidence.quote: As we go into 2022, we still believe we are a share gainer in that environment just given the strength of our portfolio and let's call it platforms that are still yet to launch across our customer set.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management's explicit share-gainer outlook into 2022 despite Intel's upcoming product launches signals confidence in AMD's competitive moat, reinforcing the positive competitive weight.

competitive.rationale: AMD continued gaining server CPU market share for the sixth consecutive quarter of record server revenue, with Milan outpacing Rome's adoption curve and management expressing confidence in Zen 4/Genoa roadmap against Intel's competitive response.

cited_evidence.quote: For the full year 2021, we now expect revenue to increase approximately 65% over 2020, driven by growth across all businesses, up from the prior guidance of 60%.
cited_evidence.reasoning: A 500bp raise to full-year revenue growth guidance, combined with a Q3 beat, is a direct credibility signal — management has consistently under-promised and over-delivered, lending high weight to the management_credibility dimension.

cited_evidence.quote: I would like to note that we will host our Accelerated Data Center Premier virtually on November 8th, with feature presentations by Dr. Lisa Su and Data Center executives Forrest Norrod and Dan McNamara.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The November 8th Accelerated Data Center Premier (the exact date of the price move) is a concrete catalyst that management telegraphed during the earnings call, directly explaining why the stock jumped +10% on that specific date — investors anticipated major data center product announcements.

cited_evidence.quote: Our product portfolio and roadmaps have never been stronger, and I look forward to sharing more details about our next-generation servers, CPUs, and GPUs at our Accelerated Data Center Premiere on November 8th.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Lisa Su's explicit framing of the November 8th event as an opportunity to reveal next-gen server/GPU details created a catalyst for the price move on that date, strongly supporting management credibility as a dimension driver.

management_credibility.rationale: Lisa Su and Devinder Kumar demonstrated strong execution credibility by beating Q3 estimates, raising full-year guidance from 60% to 65% growth, guiding Q4 above prior implied levels, and articulating a clear multi-vector growth strategy including an Accelerated Data Center Premier on November 8th.

cited_evidence.quote: Held; announced $15B/mo taper of asset purchases. First formal tightening step of the cycle.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The FOMC taper announcement was a sector-wide macro event but was widely telegraphed; it did not appear to create meaningful headwinds for AMD's stock specifically given the company-specific positive catalysts dominating on November 8th, hence a minimal neutral weight for the macro dimension.

macro.rationale: The FOMC announced a $15B/month taper on 2021-11-03, representing the first formal tightening step of the cycle, but this was broadly anticipated and had limited net directional impact specifically on AMD's move relative to the company-specific catalysts.

attribution.model_notes: live attribution: The +10.1% AMD move on 2021-11-08 is predominantly explained by the AMD Accelerated Data Center Premier event held that same day — telegraphed explicitly in the Q3 earnings call on 2021-10-26 as a venue for revealing next-generation server CPU and GPU details (MI200/CDNA2, Zen 4/Genoa roadmap). The earnings call itself delivered a Q3 beat (revenue +54% YoY, record operating income), a full-year guidance raise from 60% to 65% revenue growth, and a Q4 revenue guide above prior implied levels — all of which primed investors for the November 8th catalyst. The move is rated "mixed" because most of the structural fundamental improvement (six straight quarters of server revenue records, share gains vs. Intel) is real and durable, but the single-day +10% magnitude likely includes a transient event-day premium from the data center premiere hype. Confidence is 0.78 because the November 8th event content itself is not directly in evidence — we are inferring its positive reception from the pre-event setup, which was clearly bullish. cited_evidence.quote: Cloud revenue more than doubled year-over-year and increased sequentially as multiple hyperscalers expanded deployments of EPYC processors to power their internal properties and more than 70 new AMD instances were launched
cited_evidence.reasoning: Cloud demand doubling year-over-year and expanding to 70+ new instances is a strong positive demand signal for AMD's highest-margin Data Center segment, directly supporting the positive direction on this dimension.

cited_evidence.quote: Looking at our Embedded segment, revenue increased significantly year-over-year to a record $1.3 billion, driven by growth from aerospace and defense, industrial and communications customers. Demand across our core markets remained very strong.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Record Embedded segment revenue with broad-based demand across multiple verticals reinforces the positive demand narrative that offset PC weakness and contributed to the stock's strong move.

cited_evidence.quote: Client segment revenue was $1 billion, down 40% year-over-year, due to reduced process shipments resulting from a weak PC market and a significant inventory correction across the PC supply chain.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The 40% Client decline was severe but had been pre-announced, meaning this was already known to the market; its impact on the demand dimension is thus partially neutralized as a surprise factor.

demand.rationale: Data Center demand showed strong year-over-year growth with cloud revenue more than doubling, while PC demand weakness was already pre-announced and priced in, leaving the net demand signal positive for AMD's highest-margin segments.

cited_evidence.quote: we saw units come down, but we also saw ASPs come down on a sequential basis. Now I will say that ASPs were still up on a year-over-year basis. So we have been, let's call it, disciplined in this pricing environment.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Sequential ASP declines in Client confirm pricing pressure in that segment, though year-over-year ASPs remain positive; this mixed signal warrants a modest negative weight on the pricing dimension.

cited_evidence.quote: third quarter revenue and gross margin came in below our expectations due to softening PC demand and substantial inventory reduction actions across the PC supply chain.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The combination of inventory reduction actions and gross margin miss (driven partly by $160M in pricing/inventory charges) signals real pricing headwinds in the Client segment, supporting a negative direction for pricing.

cited_evidence.quote: We also have in the Data Center, a place cloud weightage, which has lower ASPs. So that also has an impact on margins.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management explicitly acknowledges that the cloud-heavy mix in Data Center carries lower ASPs, which creates a secondary pricing headwind even within the strong growth segment.

pricing.rationale: Client ASPs declined sequentially and inventory/pricing charges of $160M in Q3 weighed on gross margins, while Data Center cloud mix also pressured ASPs, creating a modest negative pricing signal.

cited_evidence.quote: our third-gen EPYC CPUs in market today are the highest performant and most energy efficient x86 server CPUs available, and we expect to further extend that lead with our next-generation 5-nanometer Genoa processors, which deliver significant performance, energy efficiency and TCO advantages
cited_evidence.reasoning: AMD's explicit claim of x86 server CPU leadership and the Genoa launch timing signal a strong competitive moat against Intel, a key driver of investor confidence and the stock's positive move.

cited_evidence.quote: your larger competitor now has a very sort of publicly admitted push out on their own server product. Does that make you feel like better for what you see for your Genoa ramp, especially as we get into 2023? Is it stronger than you may have seen it previously?
cited_evidence.reasoning: Intel's Sapphire Rapids delay (acknowledged in the Q2 call context) extended AMD's competitive window in server CPUs, a structural positive that market participants would weigh heavily in the November rally.

cited_evidence.quote: we feel really good about how we are positioned and the platform solutions that are coming out with all of our OEM partners. So I think on a longer term basis, the product portfolio and the product capabilities should help us continue to gain share.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Lisa Su's confidence in ongoing share gains across cloud and enterprise, supported by OEM platform launches, reinforces the positive competitive dimension and underpins the market's bullish re-rating.

competitive.rationale: AMD's competitive position in server CPUs was reinforced by the imminent Genoa launch, continued EPYC share gains across cloud hyperscalers, and Intel's Sapphire Rapids delays giving AMD an extended runway.

cited_evidence.quote: For the fourth quarter of 2022, we expect revenue to be approximately $5.5 billion, plus or minus $300 million, an increase of approximately 14% year-over-year and flat sequentially. The year-over-year growth is driven by Embedded and Data Center segments
cited_evidence.reasoning: Concrete Q4 guidance with segment-level drivers (Embedded and Data Center growth offsetting Client/Gaming declines) demonstrates management's ability to provide transparent, credible forward visibility, which is a key positive for management credibility.

cited_evidence.quote: We will prioritize the key investments for our product roadmaps and long-term growth, while taking several near-term cost management actions, including prudently controlling operating expenses and headcount growth, while actively managing inventory in line with our revenue expectations.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management's explicit commitment to balancing growth investment with near-term cost discipline signals operational maturity, positively impacting the management credibility dimension.

cited_evidence.quote: I do feel good about the products we are introducing, especially the new products and perhaps the ability to increase margins from where we are right now, guiding 51% and then taking it up from there.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Devinder Kumar's forward guidance on margin improvement trajectory provides investors with a credible path to recovery, directly bolstering management credibility as a positive factor in the stock move.

management_credibility.rationale: Management provided clear, forward-looking guidance with specific product launch timelines (Genoa, RDNA 3), articulated a path to gross margin recovery, and demonstrated disciplined cost management, all enhancing credibility.

cited_evidence.quote: Fourth straight 75bps hike to 3.75-4.00%. Statement hinted at slower pace ahead but Powell's presser reasserted higher-for-longer.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The FOMC's hint at a potential slower pace of rate hikes, though offset by Powell's hawkish tone, set the stage for the November 10 CPI-driven relief rally; the macro environment was transitioning from peak hawkishness, providing a modest positive tailwind for high-beta semiconductor stocks like AMD.

cited_evidence.quote: Our third quarter revenue and gross margin came in below our expectations due to softening PC demand and substantial inventory reduction actions across the PC supply chain. Despite the macro backdrop, overall revenue grew 29% year-over-year to $5.6 billion
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management's acknowledgment of macro headwinds while still delivering 29% revenue growth shows AMD's relative resilience, which would be amplified on a day when macro fears eased broadly, supporting a modestly positive macro contribution.

macro.rationale: The November 10 move occurred in the context of a broader CPI-driven market rally (inflation data released that day), providing a macro tailwind that amplified AMD's stock-specific catalysts.

attribution.model_notes: live attribution: The +14.3% move on 2022-11-10 was primarily driven by a combination of: (1) the November 10 US CPI print (not in evidence but well-known macro event that day) triggering a massive broad market rally, especially in beaten-down tech/semis — this macro component explains why the move exceeded what the earnings transcript alone would justify; (2) AMD's Q3 earnings (Nov 1) had pre-announced the PC miss but showed Data Center and Embedded strength; (3) Genoa launch catalyst timing created additional momentum. The vol_zscore of 4.14 and magnitude_rank of 1.0 strongly suggest the CPI surprise was the primary amplifier of a stock that already had positive earnings momentum. The predicted_return based on fundamentals alone (earnings transcript) would be ~+9%; the additional ~5% likely reflects the macro CPI surprise amplification not captured in transcript evidence. Move character is "mixed" — structural Data Center share gain story is real, but the single-day magnitude reflects transient macro relief rally dynamics.; filtered to top 50 of 1015 chunks by relevance

cited_evidence.quote: Revenue increased 42% year-over-year to $1.7 billion, led by increased adoption of our EPYC processors by cloud providers. In cloud, sales to North American hyperscalers more than doubled year-over-year
cited_evidence.reasoning: This strong YoY Data Center revenue growth demonstrates robust demand from hyperscalers, a key positive demand signal that directly supports the stock's upward move on this dimension.

cited_evidence.quote: Revenue increased significantly year-over-year to a record $1.4 billion. We had record sales across a number of our Embedded markets, including communications, automotive, industrial and healthcare, aerospace and defense
cited_evidence.reasoning: Record Embedded segment revenue across multiple end markets indicates broad-based demand strength, reinforcing the positive demand direction and contributing meaningfully to the stock's reaction.

cited_evidence.quote: we expect the overall demand environment to remain mixed with the second half stronger than the first half. In the PC market, we are planning for the PC TAM to be down approximately 10% for 2023.
cited_evidence.reasoning: While near-term demand is mixed (PC weakness, cloud inventory), the H2 inflection guidance provides a demand recovery narrative that the market likely priced in positively on the move date.

demand.rationale: Strong Data Center demand with 42% YoY revenue growth driven by hyperscaler EPYC adoption, record Embedded segment, and clear visibility into H2 recovery across segments, offsetting near-term PC and cloud inventory headwinds.

cited_evidence.quote: we do have more content with the higher core count that should also help ASPs. Bergamo will launch in the first half of the year. We are on track for the Bergamo launch

cited_evidence.quote: we are also working through our client inventory clearing. We do have more marketing programs and pricing incentives in place. We do expect that to normalize as we go through the first half of the year.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The acknowledgment of pricing programs and incentives in the Client segment is a near-term pricing headwind, dampening the overall pricing direction to mixed rather than strongly positive.

cited_evidence.quote: on the client side, we've said for the last couple of quarters that the pricing environment is more aggressive. I think that normally happens when the industry is working on rebalancing.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The aggressive Client pricing environment is a partial negative offset to the Genoa ASP uplift story, limiting the overall pricing weight for this dimension.

pricing.rationale: Genoa's higher core counts are expected to drive ASP uplift in Data Center, partially offset by aggressive pricing incentives and discounting in the Client segment as older inventory is cleared.

cited_evidence.quote: we launched our fourth gen EPYC processors this past November that deliver up to 2x faster performance in cloud, enterprise and HPC applications, and are up to 80% more energy efficient than the competition's most recently announced offerings.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The explicit performance and efficiency advantage over competition directly supports a positive competitive dimension, as it signals AMD is widening its moat in server CPUs.

cited_evidence.quote: there are more than 140 fourth-gen EPYC platforms in development from HPE, Dell, Lenovo, Super Micro and others, an increase of 40% compared to the prior generation.
cited_evidence.reasoning: A 40% increase in OEM platform support relative to the prior generation is a strong competitive signal indicating AMD is accelerating its ecosystem footprint and share capture trajectory.

cited_evidence.quote: MI300 will be the industry's first data center chip that combines a CPU, GPU and memory into a single integrated design, delivering 8x more performance and 5x better efficiency for HPC and AI workloads

competitive.rationale: AMD demonstrated continued server CPU share gains with fourth-gen EPYC (Genoa) receiving strong customer pull, 140+ OEM platforms in development, and MI300 AI accelerator positioning AMD directly in the high-growth AI compute market ahead of competition.

cited_evidence.quote: Directionally, we expect Embedded and Data Center annual revenue to grow from 2022 based on the strength of our product portfolio and expected share gains.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The directional guidance on the two highest-margin segments growing YoY in 2023 despite macro uncertainty reinforces management's credibility by setting a clear, confident forward benchmark.

cited_evidence.quote: Jean's more than 14 years of public company CFO experience and proven track record of financial leadership make her an excellent addition to our team.
cited_evidence.reasoning: The smooth CFO transition with a highly credentialed replacement (from Marvell) mitigates leadership risk that could otherwise have weighed on management credibility at a sensitive juncture.

management_credibility.rationale: Lisa Su and new CFO Jean Hu delivered a structured, credible narrative with concrete H2 recovery guidance, explicit revenue bias confirmation ('the answer is yes' on growth), disciplined expense management, and a clear AI roadmap — all reinforcing investor confidence.

cited_evidence.quote: Stepped down to 25bps to 4.50-4.75%. Powell acknowledged the disinflation process had begun.
cited_evidence.reasoning: A deceleration in the pace of rate hikes and a dovish signal on inflation is a positive macro factor for high-multiple tech stocks like AMD, providing a secondary tailwind to the earnings-driven stock move.

cited_evidence.quote: China is lifting, as they're lifting the COVID restrictions, I guess I would imagine that you would expect that ultimately, at some point, to translate into higher demand.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management acknowledged China reopening as a potential demand tailwind, adding a secondary macro positive to the overall picture even though AMD itself was cautious on quantifying the benefit.

macro.rationale: The FOMC's step-down to a 25bps hike with Powell acknowledging disinflation had begun provided a modest tailwind for growth/technology stocks on the move date, complementing AMD's earnings-driven move.

cited_evidence.reasoning: A 65% YoY client revenue decline due to inventory drawdown represents an extreme negative demand/volume outcome, weighing heavily on the demand dimension.

demand.rationale: Demand signals were deeply mixed across AMD's segments: Client revenue collapsed 65% YoY, enterprise server demand softened materially, and cloud customers were working through inventory optimization, all signaling near-term volume weakness despite embedded strength.

cited_evidence.quote: the ASPs were down quite a bit on a year-over-year basis, if you're talking about the overall Client business... given that we're undershipping in the first quarter, the ASPs are lower.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Lisa Su confirmed significant ASP declines in Client, a direct negative pricing signal that would have disappointed analysts expecting better pricing discipline.

cited_evidence.quote: I think in the Data Center market, the pricing is relatively stable. And what that comes from is our goal is to add more capability, right? So it's a TCO equation

cited_evidence.quote: the pipeline for MI300 has expanded considerably here over the last few months, and we're excited about that. We're putting a lot more resources... that's primarily to accelerate our AI software work as well as platform work.

cited_evidence.quote: In the competitions that we're having with customers, I think they're enthusiastic about Zen 4 and what it can bring into cloud workloads as well as enterprise workloads.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Customer enthusiasm for Zen 4 is a positive competitive indicator for server CPUs, partially offsetting the negative AI GPU competitive concern, keeping the overall competitive dimension mildly negative rather than strongly so.

competitive.rationale: While AMD maintained confidence in its competitive position against Intel in server CPUs, the Q1 data center results raised analyst questions about sequential share loss, and the AI GPU market remains dominated by a strong incumbent (NVIDIA) where AMD's MI300 is not yet generating revenue.

cited_evidence.quote: That was a year-over-year statement. So double-digit Data Center growth for the full year of 2023 versus 2022.

cited_evidence.quote: Based on customer demand signals, we expect second quarter revenue will be flattish sequentially with growth in our client and data center segments, offset by modest declines in our Gaming and Embedded segments.

management_credibility.rationale: Management's Q2 guidance was disappointing (flat sequentially, -19% YoY) and the full-year Data Center growth target of 'double digits' implies a mathematically aggressive ~50% H2 YoY ramp that analysts openly questioned, creating credibility risk around guidance achievability.

cited_evidence.quote: Enterprise sales declined year-over-year and sequentially as end customer demand softened due to near-term macroeconomic uncertainty.
cited_evidence.reasoning: AMD directly attributed enterprise server demand weakness to macroeconomic uncertainty, a sector-wide force that is a negative macro signal and not company-specific.

cited_evidence.quote: In the near term, we continue to see a mixed demand environment based on the uncertainties in the macro environment.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Lisa Su's acknowledgment of macro-driven mixed demand across the business confirms that sector-wide macro headwinds are a meaningful contributor to near-term weakness, supporting a negative macro attribution.

cited_evidence.quote: we're not guiding the full year just given all the puts and takes. So we see Q2 is flattish second half return to growth. We'll have to see exactly how the macro plays out across PCs and enterprise.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Refusing to guide the full year explicitly due to macro uncertainty signals that the macro environment is materially limiting management's own visibility, a negative macro dimension factor.

macro.rationale: Macroeconomic uncertainty was repeatedly cited by AMD management as the primary driver of enterprise server weakness, PC market decline, and cloud customer inventory optimization, and the company declined to provide full-year revenue guidance due to macro unpredictability.

cited_evidence.reasoning: Demand dimension: The explicit statement that the MI300 AI GPU pipeline 'expanded considerably' in recent months directly signals a step-change in forward demand from cloud AI customers, a key catalyst for the positive demand signal.

cited_evidence.quote: All of our large cloud customers have Genoa running in their data centers and are on track to begin broad deployments to power their internal workloads and public instances in the second quarter.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Demand dimension: Broad Genoa deployment across large cloud customers confirms expanding unit volumes in the high-value data center segment, reinforcing a positive demand trajectory heading into the second half of 2023.

cited_evidence.quote: We are in the very early stages of the AI computing era, and the rate of adoption and growth is faster than any other technology in recent history.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Demand dimension: Lisa Su's characterization of AI adoption velocity as unprecedented signals a structural, not cyclical, demand uplift that justifies re-rating of AMD's forward revenue potential.

demand.rationale: Surging AI/generative AI demand for MI300 GPUs, expanding cloud EPYC deployments, and a recovering client segment all signal strong forward demand inflection that investors re-rated on the move date.

cited_evidence.quote: in the Data Center market, the pricing is relatively stable. And what that comes from is -- our goal is to add more capability, right? So it's a TCO equation
cited_evidence.reasoning: Pricing dimension: Management's explicit statement that data center pricing is 'relatively stable' and supported by TCO value addition is a positive pricing signal for the highest-margin segment, limiting downside concerns.

cited_evidence.quote: when you normalize the demand and the supply, and we continue to plan a very competitive environment, so don't get us wrong on that front. But it will be better because you are not digesting the inventory
cited_evidence.reasoning: Pricing dimension: CFO Jean Hu's comment indicates client ASP pressure from inventory digestion is transient and will ease in H2, providing a moderately positive pricing outlook on a forward basis.

pricing.rationale: Data center pricing described as stable with TCO advantages supporting ASP resilience, while client ASP headwinds were acknowledged but expected to normalize in H2 2023.

cited_evidence.quote: MI300 is the first solution that has both the CPU and GPU together, and that has been very positive for the supercomputing market. I think as it relates to generative AI, and we think we have a very strong value proposition
cited_evidence.reasoning: Competitive dimension: The unique CPU+GPU integration of MI300 is positioned as a structural competitive differentiator, particularly for HPC and generative AI inference workloads, distinguishing AMD from NVIDIA-only GPU offerings.

cited_evidence.quote: we've gained share nicely over the last 4 years. When you look at our Data Center progression, it's actually been pretty steady. As we go into the second half of this year, I think we continue to believe that we have a very strong competitive position.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Competitive dimension: Lisa Su's affirmation of multi-year share gains and confidence in continued data center share gains versus Intel is a positive competitive signal that supports a higher equity valuation.

cited_evidence.quote: we feel good about our opportunities in AI. And I'd like to say that it's a multiyear journey. So this is the beginning what we think is a very significant market opportunity for us over the next 3 to 5 years.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Competitive dimension: Framing AI as a 3-5 year structural opportunity and expressing confidence in AMD's positioning suggests durable competitive relevance, supporting the magnitude of the positive price move.

competitive.rationale: AMD presented a differentiated competitive position in AI GPUs (MI300), server CPUs (Genoa/Bergamo), and articulated confidence in continued server market share gains versus Intel, while NVIDIA's dominance in AI GPUs was acknowledged but AMD framed a credible challenger narrative.

cited_evidence.quote: For the first quarter, diluted earnings per share was $0.60 due to better-than-expected revenue and operating expenses.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management credibility dimension: Q1 earnings beat on both revenue and EPS demonstrates execution against guidance, lending credibility to the more ambitious H2 and full-year targets management articulated.

cited_evidence.quote: double-digit Data Center growth is what we currently see. And certainly, we would like to ramp Genoa and Bergamo as a large piece given the strength of those products, we'd like to see them grow share here over the next couple of quarters.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management credibility dimension: The explicit quantification of 'double-digit Data Center growth' for the full year — endorsed by both Lisa Su and confirmed by CFO Jean Hu — is a strong forward commitment that materially raised the bar on investor confidence.

cited_evidence.quote: I am... [Jean Hu]: Yes, your math is right.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Management credibility dimension: Management's unambiguous endorsement of the analyst's back-of-envelope math implying ~50% H2 year-over-year data center growth is a rare and bold statement that significantly raised management credibility and investor excitement.

management_credibility.rationale: Management offered unusually specific and bold guidance — explicit double-digit full-year data center growth, Q1 beat on revenue and EPS, MI300 ramp timeline commitment, and CEO directly endorsed consensus-busting H2 implied growth math — all of which boosted investor confidence in execution.

cited_evidence.quote: In the near term, we continue to see a mixed demand environment based on the uncertainties in the macro environment. Based on customer demand signals, we expect second quarter revenue will be flattish sequentially
cited_evidence.reasoning: Macro dimension: Management acknowledged ongoing macro uncertainty and mixed near-term demand, which is a mild macro headwind, but this was broadly known and priced in; the net macro signal was neutral relative to the stock's move driver.

cited_evidence.quote: Enterprise sales declined year-over-year and sequentially as end customer demand softened due to near-term macroeconomic uncertainty.
cited_evidence.reasoning: Macro dimension: Enterprise softness tied to macro uncertainty represents a negative macro signal, but this was framed as temporary and expected to improve in H2, dampening its incremental negative impact on investor sentiment on the move date.

macro.rationale: Macro factors were acknowledged as a headwind (enterprise softness, PC TAM decline) but were not the primary driver of the move; the AI-driven demand narrative dominated sentiment on this date.

attribution.model_notes: live attribution: The -10.6% move on 2024-10-30 followed AMD's Q3 2024 earnings release (after close on 10-29).
